Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Yakov Wiegmann In Support Thereof (Motions)
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Yakov Wiegmann In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LUSINE GHAZARYAN, Esq. of Downtown L.A. Law Group (Tel: 213-389-3765) for plaintiff;
Atty. YAKOV P. WIEGMANN, Esq. of Wilson Elser Mskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) for defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s "motion to dismiss or stay action based on forum non conveniens" is denied without prejudice. The case involves an alleged assault by an Uber driver on a passenger. "On a motion for forum non conveniens, the defendant, as the moving party, bears the burden of proof." (Stangvek v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751.) Uber relies on its counsel's declaration, which is largely inadmissible and unavailing. For example, Uber (1) fails to establish the business records exception for records; (2) fails to establish that any witness would be inconvenienced by litigation in San Francisco; (3) offers only information and belief that the Uber driver resided in Nevada. (Wiegmann Dec. 1:1-2:4.)
Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Yakov Wiegmann In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Continued To Nov-19-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LUSINE GHAZARYAN, Esq. of Downtown L.A. Law Group (Tel: 213-389-3765) for plaintiff;
Atty. YAKOV P. WIEGMANN, Esq. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) for defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court and counsel for parties discuss whether the Court should consider plaintiff's untimely filed opposition.

The Court indicates it will consider plaintiff's opposition. The Court vacates its tentative ruling and continues the hearing on this matter to November 19, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Court Reporter: Vienna Nguyen, CSR No. 13137, Email: viennanguyen@gmail.com, Tel: 949-295-5737 (302/RBU)
